Q18 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
In statically determinate structures,
1. Conditions of equilibrium are sufficient to fully analyse the structure.
2. The bending moment at a section or the force in a member depends upon the material of the components of the structure.
3. Stresses are generally caused due to temperature variations.
4. Stresses are caused due to lack of fit
Which of the above statement(s) is (are) CORRECT?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
In a determinate structure, the bending moment at a section or the force in any member is
independent of the material of the components of the structure.
No stresses are caused due to temperature changes.
No stresses are caused due to lack of fit.

Q24 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A symmetrical two hinged parabolic arch when subjected to a uniformly distributed load on the entire horizontal span is subjected to

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
A symmetric parabolic arch either two hinged or three hinged is always free from bending moment
and radial shear and it is only subjected to normal thrust when it carries uniformly distributed
load over the entire span.

Q31 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following options are correct related to structural analysis?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)
Flexibility matrix is equal to its transpose as it is a square matrix and its elements are symmetrical
about diagonal.
Elements of main diagonal of stiffness matrix are always positive as displacement will always
occur in direction of force applied at a point.
Structure has to be stable and in equilibrium to develop flexibility matrix.

NAT
Correct answer: 48
Solution

48 (47 – 49)
